certificate.moduleName: ASTRO CDEM Motorola Advanced Crypto Engine (MACE)
certificate.caveat: When installed, initialized and configured as specified in Section 11 of Security Policy. No assurance of minimum security of SSPs (e.g., keys, bit strings) that are externally loaded, or of SSPs established with externally loaded SSPs
certificate.description: The ASTRO CDEM MACE provides secure key management, and data encryption.

rows: Prediction Resistance - No Mode - AES-256 Derivation Function Enabled - Yes
SP800-133rev2 Sections 4 example 1 and IG D.H
No Security Claimed. AES MAC is used as part of OTAR but is considered obfuscation.

[IG 2.4.A] P25 AES OTAR. AES MAC is applied directly to the plaintext OTAR key components and then KTS encryption is performed on the OTAR key components and decrypted within the module using AES KW Cert #5438
Caveat:Key establishment methodology provides 256 bits strength Standard:SP 800-38F IG D.G:Approved method in KW mode

Data Input Data Output Control Input Status Output
Provides an interface to the unprotected network and entry of the Crypto Officer password in encrypted form.
Data Input Data Output Control Input Status Output
This interface routes packets between subnets. The IP stack of this interface will use the subnet information to determine how to route packets between physical network interfaces.
Data Output Control Input Status Output
Provides an interface for factory programming and execution of RS232 shell commands.
Data Input Data Output Control Input Status Output
Provides an interface to the Key Variable Loader. The Traffic Encryption Key (TEK) is entered in encrypted form over the KVL interface.
Data Input Data Output Control Input Status Output
This interface provides storage for non-security related stack information.
The interface is used for zeroization of Traffic Encryption Keys (TEKs), KPK.
This interface forces a reset of the module.
The Alarm LED output is used to drive the external Alarm LED red to indicate a fatal error has been detected.
The Power LED output is used to drive the external Power LED green when power is supplied to the module.
The Ready LED output is used to drive the external Ready LED green when the module is ready to communicate with a KVL.
The TX Clear LED output is used to drive the external TX Clear LED orange when a "Bypass Rule" is programmed.

The Status LED output is used to drive the external Status LED green to indicate a good battery, and a Traffic Encryption Key (TEK) has been loaded. The Status LED output is used to drive the external Status LED yellow to indicate a good battery, but no Traffic Encryption Key (TEK) has been loaded. The Status LED output is used to drive the external Status LED red to indicate a low or dead battery.

Identity- based. Crypto-Officer Password: a 15-16 ASCII (printable) characters password is authenticated to gain access to Crypto- Officer services assocaited to the RS232 Interface. It should be noted that after authenticating , this password may be changed at any time.

The password requires a minimum of 1 Upper case, 1 Lower case, 1 Numerical and 1 special character. Since the minimum password length is 15 ASCII printable characters and there are 95 ASCII printable characters, the probability of a successful random attempt is 1 in {(10)*(262)*(32)*(9511)}, The password requires a minimum of 1 Upper case, 1 Lower case, 1 Numerical and 1 special character. Since the minimum password length is 15 ASCII printable characters and there are 95 ASCII printable characters, the probability of a successful random attempt is 1 in {(10)*(262)*(32)*(95^11) }

After the CO password has been incorrectly entered 10 consecutive times, the Module will erase all CSPs, reset the CO password back to the default and set an alarm, at which time the module must be power cycled to become operational again. The strength per minute is 10 in {(10)*(262)*(32)*(95^11) }
Identity based. Crypto-Officer Password: a 10 hexadecimal digit long password is authenticated to gain access to Crypto
The minimum password length is 10 hex digits. The probability of a successful random attempt is 10^16

After the CO password has been incorrectly entered 15 consecutive times, the Module will erase all CSPs, and set an alarm, at which time the module must be power cycled to become operational again. The strength per minute is 15x10^16.
Officer services associated to the Synchronous Interface (SSI) port. It should be noted that after authenticating , this password may be changed at any time.

Update the ASTRO CDEM MACE firmware. Firmware upgrades are authenticat ed using a digital signature. The Program Update Public Signature Key is used to validate the signature of the firmware image being loaded before it is allowed to be executed using AM2.

Covered with a hard-opaque epoxy coating that provides evidence of attempts to tamper with the ASTRO CDEM MACE.
Look for signs of tampering. Remove from service if tampering found.
Shutdown - A tamper flag is raised, a wake-up reset of the product is triggered.
Shutdown - A tamper flag is raised, a wake-up reset of the product is triggered.
Shutdown - A general reset of the chip is asserted.
Shutdown- A tamper flag is raised, a wake-up reset of the product is triggered.

CTR_DRBG internal state: V (128 bits) and Key (AES 256)
A 256-bit AES OFB (A5273) key used to decrypt keys loaded from KVL
A 256-bit AES CBC key used to decrypt downloaded firmware images.
256-bit AES-CFB8 key used for decrypting passwords during password validation
256 bit AES CFB-8 key used to encrypt all TEKs and KEKs stored in the flash.
256-bit AES-KW key used for decryption of keys in key transport operation
256-bit AES-KW key used for enabling secure communication with target devices.
AES A5273 Encryption AES A5273 Decryption
AES A5273 Encryption AES A5273 Decryption
256-bit password hash stored in the non-volatile memory.
2048-bit RSA key used to validate the signature of the firmware image during FW integrity and FW Loading before it is allowed to be executed.
A 256-bit AES CBC key used in the re-construction of IDK per SP800-133r2 (Section 6.3 #2) via XOR using IDK Block
A 256-bit AES CBC key used in the re-construction of IDK per SP800-133r2 (Section 6.3 #2) via XOR using IDK ROM
Generated with an approved RBG and pre-loaded at manufacturing

When the ASTRO CDEM MACE is powered up, the digital signature is verified.
AES-256 CTR_DRBG instantiation, generate KATs performed before the first random data generation
SHA2, -256, KAT performed before Pre-Operational FW integrity tests.
RSA-2048 SigVer, performed before Pre-Operational FW integrity tests.
Designed to quickly detect catastrophic failures that cause the noise source to become "stuck" on a single output value for a long period of time
Designed to detect a large loss of entropy that might occur as a result of some physical failure or environment al change affecting the noise source
A digital signature is generated over the code when it is built using SHA-256 and RSA-2048.
The digital signature is verified upon download into the ASTRO CDEM MACE.
The ASTRO CDEMMACE fails a KAT.

The ASTRO CDEM MACE enters the critical error state. In this state, the ASTRO CDEM MACE stores the status into the internal flash memory and then halts all further operation by entering an infinite loop.
The ASTRO CDEMMACE fails a firmware loading during program upgrade and/or firmware integrity pre-operational self- test.
The ASTRO CDEM MACE enters the firmware signature validation failure state. In this state, the ASTRO CDEM MACE halts all further operations by entering the flash programming mode.
Reboot/Power cycle the module or re-flashing a new image.
